doc:
Pure-TS port of MPFR's mpfr_const_euler_bs_2 (mpfr/src/const_euler.c L138-L180). Binary-splitting recursion for the second of two product series used in Euler-Mascheroni constant computation.

Base case (n2 - n1 == 1):
  if n1 == 0: P = 1, Q = 4N
  else:       P = (2*n1 - 1)^3, Q = 32 * n1 * N^2
  T = P

Recursive case (n2 - n1 > 1):
  m = (n1 + n2) / 2
  recurse on (n1, m) -> (P, Q, T)
  recurse on (m, n2) -> (P2, Q2, T2)
  T = T * Q2 + T2 * P
  if cont: P = P * P2
  Q = Q * Q2

The TS port takes (n1, n2, N, cont) as bigints (n1, n2, N are unsigned long counts; cont is a flag). Returns an object {P, Q, T} with the three output bigints.

This is a real algorithmic port (substantial body); the reference port faithfully recreates the recursion using native BigInt arithmetic.

Class 'misc'. Tag minimums per Rule 7 apply -- input domain is (n1, n2) pairs with n1 < n2, N a precision-derived constant, cont a flag.
c_signature: static void mpfr_const_euler_bs_2(mpz_t P, mpz_t Q, mpz_t T, unsigned long n1, unsigned long n2, unsigned long N, int cont)
divergence_from_c:
Three divergences:

  1. Visibility: C `static` -> TS internal.

  2. Return shape: C void with three mpz_t outputs -> TS object {P, Q, T}.

  3. Storage: C mpz_t -> TS bigint. Same arithmetic; different storage backend.

The cont flag controls whether P is computed in the outer level (false at the top call, true in inner recursive calls). The TS port honors this verbatim.
refs: mpfr/src/const_euler.c L138-L180 -- the C reference body.
src/internal/mpfr/ -- expected home for the substrate port.
